critic_raw:
Sepsis early-warning model — single pass:

AUROC 0.88 is a strong result on a large dataset. The comparison with the incumbent (0.80) is meaningful. However, the paper recommends moving toward deployment without providing operational evidence.

The deployment use case is threshold-triggered alerting at a specific nursing capacity constraint. AUROC does not characterize this operating point. The paper should report: (1) PPV at the threshold yielding ~2 alerts per 30-bed unit per shift, (2) alert rate per patient-day at candidate thresholds, (3) median lead time among true-positive alerts, and (4) fraction of alerts for patients already on sepsis protocols.

The patient-safety committee member is correct that these are not secondary details — they determine whether the alerting system will be clinically safe and useful.

Verdict: empirical_test_agreed
adjudication_raw: Baseline run 2. All three issues identified. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.measure: PPV and alert rate at nursing-capacity-constrained threshold
empirical_test.success_criterion: PPV clinically meaningful and alert rate within capacity
empirical_test.failure_criterion: PPV at constrained threshold below clinical utility or alert rate exceeds capacity
